attrs.alt: ART coordination cadence with Claude AI touchpoints across a Program Increment

content.text: ART Coordination in a Claude Initiative Portfolio
An Agile Release Train (ART) coordinates 50–125 people working in lockstep across 5–12 teams. When those teams build Claude-powered features, the standard SAFe ceremonies still apply, but

: shared API access creates a cross-team non-functional dependency; prompt library changes require a change-board process; and quality signals come from evaluation runs rather than deterministic test suites.

The RTE's job does not change — align teams, remove impediments, and protect the PI commitment. What changes is the data the RTE acts on: async model outputs, token-budget reports, and evaluation scores sit alongside velocity and burn-down.
Core ART Ceremonies — What Changes for Claude ARTs
Async Claude digest: aggregates 5+ stand-up notes into one prioritised blockers list
Prompt library change board: review pending cross-team prompt PRs
Evaluation harness report: show pass/fail rate for golden test set
Token budget dashboard: actual vs. allocated usage per team
Model quality retrospective: prompt regression trends, evaluation rubric updates
Daily Scrum of Scrums with Claude

The Scrum of Scrums (SoS) is a 15-minute daily ceremony where one representative per team reports on three questions: What did we complete? What are we working on? What is blocking us? With 8+ teams, the meeting overruns unless facilitated tightly. Claude transforms it into an async-first workflow.

Each team posts a short structured update to a shared Slack channel. A daily automation collects those updates and asks Claude to synthesise them into a single action-focused digest delivered to the RTE and program board.

# sos_digest.py — aggregate daily team updates into an RTE-ready digest
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class TeamUpdate:
    team: str
    completed: str
    in_progress: str
    blockers: str
    token_usage_today: int   # tokens consumed so far today

def generate_sos_digest(updates: list[TeamUpdate], daily_quota: int) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    total_used = sum(u.token_usage_today for u in updates)
    utilisation = total_used / daily_quota * 100

    update_text = "\n\n".join(
        f"**{u.team}**\n"
        f"Completed: {u.completed}\n"
        f"In progress: {u.in_progress}\n"
        f"Blockers: {u.blockers}\n"
        f"Token usage: {u.token_usage_today:,}"
        for u in updates
    )
    prompt = f"""You are an RTE assistant. Synthesise these ART daily updates into a concise SoS digest.

{update_text}

API budget: {total_used:,} / {daily_quota:,} tokens used today ({utilisation:.1f}%)

Produce a digest with three sections:
1. BLOCKERS (ranked by cross-team impact — items that block multiple teams first)
2. API BUDGET NOTE (only if utilisation > 80%)
3. WATCH LIST (items not yet blockers but trending toward risk)

Be concise. No filler. Bullet points only.
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=600,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# Example
updates = [
    TeamUpdate("Team Alpha", "Shipped AI onboarding step 3", "Writing eval cases for step 4",
               "Shared prompt library PR #47 unreviewed — blocks step 4 deploy", 410_000),
    TeamUpdate("Team Beta", "Fixed P95 latency regression", "Load testing new prompt",
               "None", 380_000),
    TeamUpdate("Team Gamma", "SOC-2 evidence 80% collected", "Writing model-selection ADR",
               "Waiting on legal sign-off for customer-data prompt template", 95_000),
    TeamUpdate("Team Delta", "Integrated Claude search in mobile app", "Sprint review prep",
               "None", 320_000),
]
digest = generate_sos_digest(updates, daily_quota=3_000_000)
print(digest)
PO Sync and Prompt Library Change Board
The PO Sync aligns Product Owners on backlog priorities and inter-team dependencies. For Claude ARTs, it doubles as a
: any team proposing a change to a shared system prompt must present a brief impact assessment and get approval before merging.

# prompt_change_board.py — evaluate impact of a proposed shared-prompt change
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class PromptChangePR:
    pr_number: int
    author_team: str
    prompt_name: str
    current_prompt: str
    proposed_prompt: str
    stated_reason: str
    consuming_teams: list[str]

def assess_prompt_change(pr: PromptChangePR) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    prompt = f"""You are a prompt review board member for an ART using shared Claude prompts.
Assess this proposed change to a shared system prompt.

Prompt name: {pr.prompt_name}
Author team: {pr.author_team}
Consuming teams: {", ".join(pr.consuming_teams)}
Reason for change: {pr.stated_reason}

CURRENT PROMPT:
{pr.current_prompt}

PROPOSED PROMPT:
{pr.proposed_prompt}

Provide:
1. DIFF SUMMARY — what changed in plain language (max 3 bullet points)
2. RISK TO CONSUMING TEAMS — specific behaviours that might change for {", ".join(pr.consuming_teams)}
3. REQUIRED TESTS — what regression tests should run before merging
4. RECOMMENDATION — Approve / Request Changes / Reject, with one-line rationale
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=700,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# Example
pr = PromptChangePR(
    pr_number=47,
    author_team="Team Alpha",
    prompt_name="onboarding-guide-v1",
    current_prompt="You are a helpful assistant guiding new users through product setup. Be concise.",
    proposed_prompt="You are a helpful assistant guiding new users through product setup. Be concise. Always ask for the user's primary use-case before proceeding to step 2.",
    stated_reason="Improves step-completion rate by personalising the flow",
    consuming_teams=["Team Beta", "Team Delta"],
)
assessment = assess_prompt_change(pr)
print(assessment)

The System Demo runs at the end of every other sprint and shows integrated, working functionality across all ART teams. For Claude features, a demo must show both the user-facing capability and the quality evidence behind it — what the evaluation harness says about accuracy, latency, and safety.

# demo_eval_report.py — generate quality evidence for the System Demo
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class FeatureEvalResult:
    feature_name: str
    team: str
    test_cases_run: int
    pass_rate: float          # 0.0 – 1.0
    p95_latency_ms: int
    human_eval_score: float   # 1.0 – 5.0; None if not yet run
    known_regressions: list[str]

def generate_demo_quality_report(results: list[FeatureEvalResult]) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    results_text = "\n\n".join(
        f"Feature: {r.feature_name} ({r.team})\n"
        f"Test cases: {r.test_cases_run} | Pass rate: {r.pass_rate:.0%} | P95: {r.p95_latency_ms}ms\n"
        f"Human eval: {r.human_eval_score or 'not run'}/5.0\n"
        f"Regressions: {', '.join(r.known_regressions) or 'none'}"
        for r in results
    )
    prompt = f"""Generate a concise System Demo quality briefing for ART stakeholders.
Summarise the evaluation results below in plain language suitable for a 5-minute briefing slot.

{results_text}

Structure:
1. OVERALL QUALITY SIGNAL (2 sentences — what the numbers say collectively)
2. FEATURE HIGHLIGHTS (features that exceeded quality targets — brief)
3. WATCH ITEMS (features below 90% pass rate or with regressions — what the team is doing about it)
4. DEMO RISK NOTE (anything that might cause a live demo to fail)
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=600,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

results = [
    FeatureEvalResult("AI Onboarding Wizard", "Team Alpha", 120, 0.94, 820, 4.2, []),
    FeatureEvalResult("Knowledge Search", "Team Beta", 80, 0.87, 950, None, ["returns empty on 3-word queries"]),
    FeatureEvalResult("Ticket Deflection", "Team Gamma", 200, 0.96, 540, 4.5, []),
    FeatureEvalResult("Mobile AI Assistant", "Team Delta", 60, 0.91, 1100, 3.8, []),
]
report = generate_demo_quality_report(results)
print(report)
Inspect and Adapt — Quantitative PI Assessment

The Inspect and Adapt (I&A) workshop closes each PI. Teams measure actual vs. planned business value delivered, run a retrospective, and build an improvement backlog. For Claude ARTs, the quantitative assessment adds
alongside the standard velocity and feature-completion measures.
Features with passing eval harness / total shipped
Prompt regressions detected post-sprint (caught by golden set)
P95 Claude API response time per feature
Business Value delivered vs. PI plan
Evaluation score delta (start vs. end of PI)
Token budget utilisation efficiency (value / token cost)

# ia_clustering.py — cluster retrospective data and propose improvement backlog
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class RetroItem:
    team: str
    category: str   # "went-well" | "improvement" | "action"
    text: str

def cluster_retro_items(items: list[RetroItem]) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    improvements = [i for i in items if i.category == "improvement"]
    improvement_text = "\n".join(
        f"[{i.team}] {i.text}" for i in improvements
    )
    prompt = f"""Analyse these ART retrospective improvement items from a Claude AI release train.

{improvement_text}

1. THEME CLUSTERS — group into 3-5 root-cause themes (name each theme; list contributing items)
2. SYSTEMIC vs. LOCAL — identify which themes are ART-wide vs. team-specific
3. IMPROVEMENT BACKLOG — for each systemic theme, propose one concrete improvement story
   (format: "As an ART, we want to X so that Y")
4. QUICK WINS — two improvements that can be implemented in Sprint 1 of the next PI with low effort
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=900,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

retro_items = [
    RetroItem("Team Alpha", "improvement", "Prompt library PR reviews take 4+ days; blocks sprint work"),
    RetroItem("Team Beta", "improvement", "No shared standard for what a 'passing' eval score means"),
    RetroItem("Team Gamma", "improvement", "Token budget reports come too late — we exceed quota before warning"),
    RetroItem("Team Delta", "improvement", "Prompt library PR reviews block our sprint demos too"),
    RetroItem("Team Alpha", "improvement", "Evaluation harness not run consistently — some features shipped untested"),
    RetroItem("Team Beta", "improvement", "No process for deciding when to upgrade to a new Claude model"),
    RetroItem("Team Gamma", "improvement", "Legal review of prompts is a recurring blocker — same pattern every sprint"),
    RetroItem("Team Delta", "improvement", "Evaluation harness results are not visible in System Demo automatically"),
]
analysis = cluster_retro_items(retro_items)
print(analysis)
The RTE Sync brings together the Release Train Engineer, System Architect, Business Owners, and team representatives once a week to address program-level impediments. For Claude ARTs, the

# rte_sync_report.py — weekly ART health report for the RTE Sync
import anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date

@dataclass
class TeamWeeklyMetrics:
    team: str
    tokens_used_this_week: int
    weekly_quota: int
    features_in_progress: int
    features_completed_this_pi: int
    open_blockers: list[str]
    eval_pass_rate: float | None   # None if no eval run this week

def generate_rte_sync_report(
    metrics: list[TeamWeeklyMetrics],
    sprint_num: int,
    sprint_total: int,
) -> str:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    metrics_text = "\n\n".join(
        f"**{m.team}**\n"
        f"Token usage: {m.tokens_used_this_week:,} / {m.weekly_quota:,} "
        f"({m.tokens_used_this_week/m.weekly_quota*100:.0f}%)\n"
        f"Features completed this PI: {m.features_completed_this_pi} | In progress: {m.features_in_progress}\n"
        f"Eval pass rate: {f'{m.eval_pass_rate:.0%}' if m.eval_pass_rate else 'not run'}\n"
        f"Blockers: {'; '.join(m.open_blockers) or 'none'}"
        for m in metrics
    )

    prompt = f"""Generate a weekly RTE Sync briefing for a Claude AI release train.
Today is week {sprint_num} of {sprint_total} sprints in this PI.

{metrics_text}

Structure the briefing as:
1. PROGRAM HEALTH SIGNAL — one paragraph: is the ART on track?
2. TOKEN BUDGET STATUS — highlight any team over 85% weekly quota
3. QUALITY TRENDS — eval pass rates; flag anything below 90%
4. TOP 3 IMPEDIMENTS — ranked by blast radius; who is blocked by whom
5. RECOMMENDED RTE ACTIONS — specific, this-week actions to take
    """

    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=800,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return response.content[0].text

# Example: mid-PI week 3 of sprint 2
weekly = [
    TeamWeeklyMetrics("Team Alpha", 950_000, 1_000_000, 3, 7, ["Prompt PR #47 still unreviewed"], 0.94),
    TeamWeeklyMetrics("Team Beta",  720_000, 1_000_000, 2, 5, [], 0.87),
    TeamWeeklyMetrics("Team Gamma", 180_000,   500_000, 4, 3, ["Legal sign-off delayed 1 week"], None),
    TeamWeeklyMetrics("Team Delta", 610_000,   500_000, 2, 6, [], 0.91),
]
report = generate_rte_sync_report(weekly, sprint_num=3, sprint_total=10)
print(report)
Not every impediment belongs at the RTE level. Claude helps triage which blockers require executive involvement versus which the RTE can resolve through direct team coordination.

# impediment_triage.py — classify blockers by resolution level
import anthropic
from enum import Enum

class EscalationLevel(Enum):
    TEAM       = "Team (self-resolve)"
    RTE        = "RTE (program-level)"
    EXECUTIVE  = "Executive (portfolio-level)"

def classify_impediment(description: str, affected_teams: list[str]) -> dict:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    prompt = f"""Classify this ART impediment for a Claude AI release train.

Impediment: {description}
Teams affected: {", ".join(affected_teams)}

Respond with JSON only:
{{
  "escalation_level": "Team | RTE | Executive",
  "rationale": "one sentence",
  "suggested_owner": "role name",
  "urgency": "this-sprint | next-sprint | backlog"
}}
    """
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=200,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    import json
    return json.loads(response.content[0].text)

# Examples
blockers = [
    ("Shared prompt library PR unreviewed for 5 days", ["Team Alpha", "Team Delta"]),
    ("Legal refuses to approve customer-data prompt template without CISO sign-off", ["Team Gamma"]),
    ("API rate limit quota insufficient for all teams during peak demo hours", ["Team Alpha", "Team Beta", "Team Gamma", "Team Delta"]),
]
for desc, teams in blockers:
    result = classify_impediment(desc, teams)
    print(f"Impediment: {desc[:50]}...")
    print(f"  -> {result.get('escalation_level')} | {result.get('urgency')} | Owner: {result.get('suggested_owner')}")
    print()
Common Anti-Patterns in Claude ART Coordination
Teams over-allocate quota at PI Planning; actual usage is 40% of commitment
Set per-sprint budgets with real-time dashboard; allow burst requests via RTE
Teams fork shared prompts rather than going through the change board
Change board seen as slow bureaucracy
SLA: change board decision within 2 business days; async review via Claude
Eval harness runs pass but real-user quality is poor
Golden test set not updated to reflect actual user queries
Quarterly test-set refresh; include recent failure cases
Live Claude demo fails due to rate limits during the System Demo
All teams demo simultaneously; no capacity reservation
Pre-warm reserved capacity; stagger demo slots 15 min apart
Team upgrades to latest model mid-sprint without regression testing
Individual team decision, not ART decision
Architecture guild owns model upgrade decisions; upgrade windows in IP sprint only
A Claude ART needs a small set of lightweight automation tools in place before the first PI launches. These are not heavyweight platforms — each can be a Python script or simple webhook.
: collects team stand-up notes from Slack/Teams; posts Claude-generated digest to #rte-sync channel by 09:30 daily
: real-time API usage per team; alert threshold at 85% of weekly quota; visible in the ART's shared workspace
: git-backed YAML/JSON store; version-tagged system prompts; PR-based change process with two-reviewer requirement
: runs golden test set on every shared-prompt PR; blocks merge if pass rate drops below 90%
: automated API quota reservation for System Demo window; released automatically 1 hour after demo end
: aggregates per-team velocity, eval scores, token efficiency, and escaped-defect counts into one PI metrics dashboard before the I&A workshop

ART coordination for Claude initiative portfolios preserves every standard SAFe ceremony but adds AI-specific data layers at each touchpoint: an async Claude digest replaces or compresses the Scrum of Scrums stand-up; a prompt library change board sits inside the PO Sync; evaluation harness results appear alongside working software at System Demos; and the Inspect and Adapt workshop extends its quantitative assessment to model quality, evaluation coverage, and token-budget efficiency. The RTE's coordination burden increases modestly — but with Claude handling the synthesis work (aggregating blockers, assessing prompt changes, clustering retro themes), the program's signal-to-noise ratio improves significantly.
